data.title: Tesla lands a Street-high price target from Wedbush on huge AI opportunity
data.description:
Wedbush Securities hiked its price target on Outperform-rated Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) to $600 to reflect the firm's view that an accelerated AI path for the company is now on the horizon. Analyst Dan Ives noted that investors are underestimating the transformation underway at the company. 

"We believe Tesla is taking major steps in advancing its AI Revolution path with autonomous and robotics front and center heading into 2026 that will be a game changer and define Tesla's future," wrote Ives. 

Ives and his team estimate the AI and autonomous opportunity is worth at least $1 trillion alone for Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA). The expectation is that under a Trump White House over the coming year, the key initiatives will now get fast-tracked as the federal regulatory spiderweb that the company has encountered over the past few years around FSD/autonomy.

The Wedbush view is that Tesla (TSLA) could reach a $2 trillion market cap in early 2026 in a bull case scenario and $3 trillion by the end of 2026 as full-scale volume production begins of the autonomous and robotics roadmap. 

Shares of Tesla (TSLA) fell 0.7% in premarket trading on Friday after a 4.4% decline on Thursday when the electric vehicle maker was reported to have seen a slump in European sales again.

Looking ahead, Tesla (TSLA) will issue its third-quarter deliveries report during the first few days of October and will release its third-quarter earnings report later in the month.

data.title: Crinetics wins FDA approval for acromegaly therapy
data.description:
Crinetics Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:CRNX) has received approval for its lead asset, Palsonify (paltusotine), as a first-line treatment for adults with acromegaly, a rare condition characterized by uneven growth of bones and tissues due to excessive secretion of growth hormone.

Accordingly, the once-daily oral therapy will be available in the U.S. to treat acromegaly patients who have failed to respond adequately to surgical interventions or for whom surgery is not a treatment option.

The approval is backed by the company’s PATHFNDR-1 and PATHFNDR-2 Phase 3 pivotal trials, which indicated, among other outcomes, that patients on Palsonify experienced a significant reduction of signs and symptoms linked to the condition.

The company plans to launch the product in early October. Its marketing authorization application for Palsonify is currently under review in the EU, where an expert panel of the drug regulator, the European Medicines Agency (MEA),  is expected to weigh in on the MAA in H1 2026.

data.title: Soligenix stock down after pricing $7.5M public offering to raise capital
data.description:
Soligenix (NASDAQ:SNGX) has announced the pricing of a "reasonable best efforts" public offering to existing and select healthcare-focused institutional investors, that includes the sale of 5,555,560 shares of common stock (or equivalents) and accompanying warrants to purchase an equal number of shares, priced at a combined $1.35 per share and warrant unit.

The gross proceeds from the offering are expected to be approximately $7.5 million.

The warrants will have an exercise price of $1.35 per share, become immediately exercisable, and expire five years from the date of issuance.

The company is also amending 1,162,064 shares of existing warrants (dated May 2023, April 2024, and July 2024). These Existing Warrants will now have an exercise price of $1.35 per share and will expire concurrently with the warrants issued in this new public offering.

The company intends to use the net proceeds of this offering to fund research and development and commercialization activities, working capital and general corporate purposes.

The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about September 29, 2025.

Stock of SNGX were down 23.5% in premarket trading on Friday.

data.title: Oil slips but heads for biggest weekly gain in 3 months on geopolitical tensions
data.description:
Oil futures ticked lower on Friday but were set for their biggest weekly gain in three months, driven by heightened geopolitical tensions after Ukraine’s attacks on Russia’s energy infrastructure prompted Moscow to curb fuel exports.

WTI crude (CL1:COM) fell 0.4% to $64.75/bbl, while Brent (CO1:COM) dipped 0.4% to $69.18. Both benchmarks are set to register their biggest increases since mid-June.

Russia will introduce a partial ban on diesel exports until the end of the year and extend an existing ban on gasoline exports, Deputy Prime Minister Alexander Novak said on Thursday.

Meanwhile, European diplomats concluded that a recent Russian incursion into Estonia was a deliberate tactic ordered by Russian commanders. They subsequently warned that NATO is ready to respond to any further violations of its airspace with full force, including shooting down Russian planes, echoing previous comments by President Trump.

"This ratchets up the tensions in the Russia-Ukraine war and raised the prospect of increased pressure on Russia via additional sanctions on its oil industry," ANZ analysts said in a note.

European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen also warned that she’s looking to end Russian oil purchases by the end of the year.

On the supply side, oil flow from Iraq’s Kurdistan region to Turkey will resume on Saturday, two Iraqi oil ministry officials told Reuters on Thursday.

"These geopolitical tensions reversed earlier losses after a landmark agreement was reached to allow the resumption of exports from Iraq’s Kurdistan, which could return up to 500 kb/d to the global market."

 

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG), (XLE)

data.description:

data.title: TikTok U.S. valued at $14B in proposed deal, below analyst estimates
data.description:
President Donald Trump has cleared a proposal to allow TikTok to continue operating in the U.S., in a deal that Vice President JD Vance said would value the app's U.S. operations at around $14B.

Under the proposed deal, TikTok’s U.S. business will be majority-owned by an American investor group. Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) will serve as TikTok's security provider.

The full composition of the investor group is yet to be finalized. Oracle (NYSE:ORCL), Silver Lake and Abu Dhabi-based MGX will reportedly be the main investors, while ByteDance (BDNCE) will hold less than 20% in TikTok U.S.

"Ultimately, the investors are going to make the determination about what they want to invest in and what they think is the proper value," Vance said, who also noted that there was "some resistance on the Chinese side."

The $14B valuation is well below some analyst estimates. In April, Wedbush analyst Dan Ives estimated that TikTok was worth around $100B with the algorithm, or $30B-$40B without it.

The proposed deal "could be the most undervalued tech acquisition of the decade," Ashwin Binwani, founder of Alpha Binwani Capital, told Bloomberg. He estimated that the $14B valuation reflects only a third of TikTok's true value.

"By every major financial metric and peer comparison, this price tag looks dramatically misaligned with reality," he added.

Trump's executive order provides a 120-day window to complete the deal. It's unclear if China will approve the proposal.

data.title: Concentrix drops 21% as focus remains on margin recovery, tariff impact
data.description:
Shares of Concentrix (NASDAQ:CNXC) dropped 21% in premarket trading as the technology and services company fell short of estimates for quarterly profit, even as revenue surpassed expectations, while investors focused on margin recovery and investment returns amid tariff headwinds. 

The company's guidance for full-year revenue and EPS narrowed and was slightly reduced versus the previous quarter, and margin improvement is now projected as a multi-quarter process rather than immediate.

Margin shortfalls in Q3 were attributed to excess capacity from tariff-related client impacts and accelerated transformation programs, whereas Q2 margin softness was mainly due to labor retention during client pauses.

"And what happened was, with some of the additional noise with tariffs within the third quarter, by the second month, we still weren't seeing that uptick coming through from the clients. The clients weren't seeing that uptick either," CEO Christopher Caldwell said.

"There was a bit of additional noise before August on tariffs, frankly."

The company's management, however, conveyed that revenue growth outpaced guidance based on AI-driven integrated offerings and continued market share gains.

Management remains confident in the long-term strategy and expects the iX suite of software to become accretive as the year ends, while capital returns to shareholders are maintained through an increased dividend and ongoing share repurchases. 

Ahead of the earnings, Agar Capital noted that Concentrix (NASDAQ:CNXC) remains undervalued, trading at just 4.4x forward earnings despite strong fundamentals, robust cash flow, and operational AI solutions.

data.title: Lexaria Bioscience stock soars after ending $5M stock sale agreement with JonesTrading
data.description:
• Lexaria Bioscience (NASDAQ:LEXX) rose 32% in premarket trading on Friday after the company announced the termination of its Capital on-Demand Sales Agreement with JonesTrading Institutional Services LLC, effective September 19.
  • As of the date of termination, the company had sold an aggregate of 14,995 shares under the agreement for gross proceeds of $38,236.00.
  • The deal, originally signed on August 21, 2024, and amended on February 5, 2025, had authorized Lexaria (NASDAQ:LEXX) to sell up to $5 million worth of its common stock through JonesTrading, acting as either sales agent or principal.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: INTC, GFS, and more
data.description:
Stock futures recorded slight gains in premarket trading Friday, with market attention squarely focused on U.S. PCE inflation data set for release later today, which could influence the Federal Reserve’s next policy steps.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) +4%, GlobalFoundries (NASDAQ:GFS) +4% - Chipmakers rallied after the WSJ reported the Trump administration is weighing a policy requiring U.S. firms to manufacture as many chips domestically as they import. Companies falling short of the 1:1 ratio could face tariffs, the report said.

Biggest stock losers

  • Concentrix (NASDAQ:CNXC) -21% – Shares plunged after Q3 results showed a profit miss despite largely in-line revenue. Adjusted EBITDA margin fell 180 bps Y/Y. Q4 revenue is projected at $2.525B–$2.55B (vs. $2.53B consensus), with non-GAAP EPS of $2.85–$2.96, missing the $3.32 estimate. Full-year revenue guidance of $9.798B–$9.823B was in line, but EPS of $11.11–$11.23 fell short of the $11.69 consensus.

data.title: Li Auto launches Li i6, a five-seat battery electric SUV
data.description:
• Li Auto (NASDAQ:LI) on Friday officially launched Li i6, a five-seat battery electric SUV.
  • Li i6 is priced at RMB 249,800 for its standard configuration. Deliveries of Li i6 will commence on September 27, 2025.
